rag_rules: Fix unresolved references before adding AGREE clauses; remove invalid guarantees if root cause cannot be resolved.
Use only visible AADL features or locally declared helpers in AGREE expressions to avoid type errors.
Place AGREE contracts in valid component type or implementation scopes based on the scope of referenced elements.
generic_rules: When AGREE reports type mismatches, ensure all expressions and declarations use compatible scalar types (int, real, bool).
For component type contracts, avoid using implementation-only subcomponent paths; use visible inputs and outputs.
If a guarantee or assumption references an invalid type or unresolved identifier, remove the clause or fix the reference.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be placed inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks in appropriate component scopes.
AGREE scalar types are int, real, and bool; avoid referencing AADL package-qualified types not present in the model.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types that are not declared in the AADL model or its dependencies.
Do not write guarantees or assumptions that reference unresolved identifiers or invalid helper declarations.
